commits.message: feat(scroll-signup): add to how-to guide pages on all 3 sites

feat(blog): add 6 weeks of building with Claude Code post + content drafts
fix(scroll-signup): persist subscription before form submit, add dismiss button, reduce timer
fix(nio-blog): restore 14 broken posts + prevent claude tool use in generator

wip(reddit): Reddit Growth Playbook page + evidence screenshots
fix(subscribe): use PostHog server-side capture instead of filesystem
wip(reddit): add comments guide with 6 comment types + tabbed UI
feat(subscribe): add Telegram + Google Sheet notifications on signup
wip(reddit): integrate Reddit brand assets into page design
feat(reddit): text-first evidence cards with screenshot toggle
refactor(reddit): restructure page flow — types → rules → receipts

dev_equivalent.explanation: A mid-level developer writes ~150 lines of production code per day at ~$500/day (fully loaded). Today's 4,978 net lines represent approximately 33 developer-days of output — what a small team would produce in 2+ month.

assumptions.basis: Industry average for meaningful, reviewed, production-quality code
